design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate linear platforming stages while managing limited spell resources; defeat stage bosses using boomerang attacks and unlocked spells to progress through Lovecraftian-themed narrative
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (spell attacks) combined with execution-heavy platforming challenges; speed vs. precision in combat encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as a sorceress wielding magical spells and boomerangs to stop an occult conspiracy in 1920s New York; embody a proactive female protagonist fighting supernatural threats
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising difficulty paired with fast-paced, speed-focused gameplay; rejects accessibility in favor of demanding player execution and memorization; embraces genre anachronisms (denim-clad bikers with scimitars) as intentional design humor

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; difficulty is fixed and non-adjustable. Players gain agency through spell unlocks that provide tactical options for boss encounters

progression.upgrade_system: Spell acquisition system; player gains access to up to five different spell attacks across progression, each providing distinct tactical advantages
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage completion unlocks new spells; each level ends in boss fight that gates progression
replayability_hooks: Challenge-focused design encourages replaying stages to master execution
Fast-paced gameplay and 'surprisingly addictive' nature creates continuous play motivation
Spell variety incentivizes experimenting with different tactical approaches on subsequent playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Explicit Lovecraftian Cthulhu Mythos references (Hastur awakening, ancient bloodlines)
Historical setting integration (1920s New York, references to Al Capone/Vincente DeMarcoto)
Character relationships (assistance from Earnest Evans, protagonist Anett Myer's heritage as sorceress)
Environmental storytelling through stage themes and boss encounters
Cross-game narrative continuity (sequel to Earnest Evans, prequel to Anett Futatabi)

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage culminates in boss encounter designed as skill check; bosses test mastery of movement, timing, and spell resource management accumulated during stage progression

faction_indicators: Cult led by Henry attempting to resurrect Hastur
Sorceress bloodline descendants (protagonist Anett Myer and allies)
danger_signals: Organized crime collaboration with occult forces
environmental_storytelling: 1920s New York urban setting hosting occult conspiracy
Genre-defying visual design choices (anachronistic enemy aesthetics)
Stage progression through locations of supernatural significance
